title: potentilla arenaria subsp. incana
intro: Potentilla arenaria subsp. incana, also known as the hairy cinquefoil, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This perennial herb is native to Europe and Asia, and is known for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Potentilla arenaria subsp. incana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and ointments for topical and internal use.
